domains.description: Mathematics as the main subject: theorem proving, equation solving, symbolic manipulation, topology, calculus, trigonometry, probability, statistics as mathematical method, distributions, matrix/vector operations, optimization, and mathematical problem solving.
positive_examples: A proof involving sine, cosine, tangent, identities, functions, or equations.
A topology passage about nowhere dense sets, closure, complete spaces, or uniform norm.
A statistics lesson about median, mean, skew, probability, MAPE, model error, or distributions where the mathematical object is central.
negative_notes: Do not assign math to physics, chemistry, biology, or engineering text merely because it contains formulas, units, or numeric calculations.
Do not assign math to software documentation about matrix commands when the software/API behavior is the main subject.
If formulas explain a natural phenomenon such as force, chemistry, molecules, climate, or animal adhesion, prefer natural_science.

domains.description: Natural science as the main subject: biology, chemistry, physics, earth science, astronomy, ecology, climate science, geology, genetics, species, habitats, molecules, atoms, forces, materials as natural phenomena, and empirical scientific findings.
positive_examples: A biology passage about species, genes, cells, animals, rainforests, ecology, or endangered habitats.
A chemistry passage about atoms, molecules, valence electrons, bromine, iodine, sucrose, or chemical formulas.
A physics passage about force, Newtons, mirrors, adhesion, motion, or experimental measurement of a natural system.
negative_notes: Do not assign natural_science to pure math/statistics notation with no natural phenomenon.
Do not assign natural_science to engineering patents or devices unless the natural phenomenon is the main subject.
Medical diagnosis, treatment, symptoms, mental health, and public health belong to medicine_health.

domains.description: Software and computing as the main subject: programming, code, APIs, algorithms as software implementation, data structures, computational complexity, packages, libraries, CLIs, configuration, servers, databases, cybersecurity, developer documentation, and operating systems.
positive_examples: API or package documentation with functions, classes, parameters, commands, or configuration options.
A programming explanation about B-trees, algorithms, complexity, code, data structures, or database behavior.
A web server or software product page where server configuration, reverse proxy behavior, SSL/TLS, or software features are central.
negative_notes: Do not assign software to physical devices, patent apparatus, terminals, sensors, circuits, adapters, printers, POS hardware, or industrial systems unless code/software is clearly the main subject.
Do not assign software only because a patent text mentions a program, data, display, keyboard, LAN, or computer inside a physical system.
Mathematical use of software tools may be math when the math object is central.

domains.description: Engineered physical or cyber-physical systems as the main subject: patents, devices, apparatuses, circuits, terminals, sensors, adapters, data taps, printers, keyboards, displays, POS systems, electronics, appliances, vehicles, infrastructure, telecommunications hardware, industrial systems, and manufacturing processes.
positive_examples: A patent-like description of a point-of-sale terminal, LAN adapter, data tap, printer connection, display, keyboard, invoice capture, transaction hardware, or circuit.
A technical description of sensors, adapters, apparatuses, appliances, electronics, terminals, vehicles, engines, or infrastructure.
A product or engineering specification for industrial equipment, physical hardware, or a cyber-physical system.
negative_notes: Do not assign engineering_technology to pure software APIs, code snippets, packages, CLI documentation, or algorithm tutorials.
A computer mentioned as a component of a patented physical system does not automatically make the topic software.
Consumer lifestyle reviews of gadgets may be lifestyle_everyday when practical consumer experience dominates.

domains.description: Medicine, clinical care, mental health, disease, symptoms, treatments, patient education, public health, disability, rehabilitation, anatomy, drugs, healthcare systems, and health advice where health outcomes are central.
positive_examples: A mental health help article about depression, suicide, therapy, medication, or emergency care.
A medical genetics article about disease risk, autism, schizophrenia, symptoms, or clinical findings.
A rehabilitation or disability technology program framed around healthcare and patient outcomes.
negative_notes: Biology or chemistry without clinical/health framing is natural_science.
Fitness, fashion, or everyday wellness without medical claims is lifestyle_everyday.
Engineering devices belong to engineering_technology unless the clinical health context is dominant.

domains.description: Business, marketing, services, finance, economics, markets, accounting, management, corporate strategy, employment, pricing analysis, trade, supply chains, and economic mechanisms as the subject.
positive_examples: An explanation of services marketing, intangible services, service quality, consumer evaluation, or marketing management.
A finance or economics passage about markets, interest rates, pricing, accounting, investment portfolios, or demand.
A business operations article about hiring, management, supply chains, or corporate strategy.
negative_notes: Do not assign business_economics simply because a page is commercial or sells a product; product_commercial is genre/function, not semantic topic.
A clothing storefront is usually lifestyle_everyday plus commercial function, not business_economics.
Tax law and government regulation usually belong to law_government_policy.

domains.description: Law, public policy, government, elections, civic institutions, courts, regulation, legal rights, sovereignty, international governance, war policy, public administration, taxation, assessment systems, and official policy processes.
positive_examples: A passage about elections, presidential election resources, legislation, public policy, or government reform.
A discussion of war, sovereignty, international courts, nuclear proliferation, or global governance.
A local or national government text about taxes, assessment systems, public services, compliance, or regulation.
negative_notes: Historical narrative without policy/institutional focus may be humanities_arts.
Social inequality analysis may be social_sciences when empirical society/behavior framing dominates.
Do not assign this label for every mention of a country, official, or event without legal/government/policy substance.

domains.description: Human society and behavior studied or analyzed through sociology, psychology, anthropology, education research, demographics, communication studies, gender studies, social inequality, public opinion, development studies, and disaster/social vulnerability analysis.
positive_examples: A sociology or equity passage about immigrant integration, gender inequality, poverty, labor, or social conditions.
A development studies or disaster-risk analysis about social vulnerability, disaster traps, and institutional effects.
A psychology or social behavior passage about groups, motivation, memory, or public opinion.
negative_notes: Government rules, elections, legal institutions, and policy mechanisms belong to law_government_policy when they are central.
History, religion, biography, art, literature, and philosophy usually belong to humanities_arts.
Casual life advice or personal narrative is usually lifestyle_everyday unless social-science analysis dominates.

domains.description: Humanities and arts as the subject: history, religion, literature, language, dictionaries, philosophy, art history, artists, biography, cultural analysis, music, film studies, ethics, and interpretive or historical exposition.
positive_examples: A passage about Picasso, Miro, Dali, surrealism, art exhibitions, painting, or artist biography.
A dictionary or language-history passage about the OED, word meanings, pronunciation, or literary language.
A religious, philosophical, historical, literary, or biographical reference entry.
negative_notes: Casual lifestyle, parenting, sports, food, training, or personal advice is lifestyle_everyday unless arts/culture/history analysis is central.
Empirical social inequality or sociology belongs to social_sciences.
Election law, government institutions, war policy, and sovereignty belong to law_government_policy when institutional/policy substance dominates.

domains.description: Everyday life, hobbies, food, cooking, restaurants, sports, fitness, parenting, clothing, fashion, consumer goods, travel, home advice, local accidents/events, personal routines, practical advice, and non-specialist consumer experience.
positive_examples: A clothing or boots product page, fashion description, consumer buying note, or practical product description.
A food, chef, restaurant, sports, rowing, training, parenting, camp, home, or personal routine article.
A local accident report or everyday community event with no stronger policy, science, or specialist topic.
negative_notes: Historical, literary, religious, artistic, philosophical, or biographical analysis belongs to humanities_arts.
Medical diagnosis, symptoms, treatment, and public health belong to medicine_health.
Physical hardware specifications or automotive engineering details may be engineering_technology when the technical artifact is central.

domains.description: Use only when the semantic topic is absent, too short, too noisy, too mixed, outside the taxonomy, or not recoverable with reasonable confidence.
positive_examples: SEO or spam-like fragments with broken text and no reliable subject.
Navigation-only or boilerplate-only chunks.
Very short mixed snippets where no domain dominates.
negative_notes: Do not use other_unclear when a concrete semantic topic is visible.
Do not use other_unclear as a generic low-confidence prediction for hard but recoverable math, science, software, engineering, humanities, policy, or lifestyle text.
Quality/noise and genre/function should be tracked separately from semantic topic.
